cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Atomic lockstep
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: Commits update JSON + narrative + ENFORCEMENT-MAP + CLAUDE.md atomically.

cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Promotion framework
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: Tier 1 advisory + promotion_criterion. Clean data → Tier 2; none → retire.

cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Self-healing boundary
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: Mechanical drift auto-fixes; semantic drift self-flags.

cross_cutting_meta_rules.name: Layer-3 Overlay Collision Tiebreaker
cross_cutting_meta_rules.rule_text: When adopter Layer-3 overlay and foundation canonical both declare the same rule ID, archetype enum value, or extensible entry kind: adopter Layer-3 SHADOWS foundation (adopter wins). Foundation declaration is preserved but does not fire. Adopter MUST carry `_override_reason` (free-text, mandatory) on every shadowing entry. Write-time enforcement via pre-write-guard.sh: shadowing writes without `_override_reason` are DENIED at write-time. Per-write `--force-override` flag bypasses the DENY for a single write; no persistent disable. Adopter renames are special-case shadows under a new name (no separate `_rename_history` field; adopter manifest is canonical naming). Foundation upgrades touching shadowed entries surface as merge conflicts in the adopter's vault repo plus `foundation-upgrade-touches-shadowed-entry` warning from librarian governance-parity-audit (audit-time, because foundation upgrades are not adopter writes). ADR-0006 documents the design rationale + 6 rejected alternatives (foundation-wins / newest-timestamp-wins / hard-rename-required / schema-side-disjoint-namespaces / audit-time-only / persistent-disable-config).
cross_cutting_meta_rules.failure_mode: Silent collisions between adopter customization and foundation canonical produce unpredictable runtime behavior — rules with the same ID compete, archetype lookups fail, overlay edits silently disable foundation rules. Without the tiebreaker, every foundation upgrade is a potentially-breaking change for adopters with overlays on those entries; without write-time DENY, adopters discover collisions days later in audit reports rather than at the write itself. Write-time enforcement plus the upgrade-time audit finding close both gaps without forcing adopters into rename-or-refuse-upgrade choices.
enforcement_layer: pre-write-guard.sh write-time DENY on shadowing writes missing `_override_reason`
version-control merge-conflict surfacing on foundation upgrades touching shadowed entries
